data.content:
# Implementation Handoff Capsule — knots-41ba

## Artifact
- Branch: worktree-knots-41ba-scope-metadata
- Commit: 384fc278f094d09a36bfe8e68fdc803dac3d73de
- Remote: github.com:acartine/knots (pushed)

## What this step delivers
Verification that all ten scope metadata fields are first-class optional
fields on the ScopeData domain type, with no overlap with any existing
estimate/minutes concept. The implementing code landed via the child knots
(knots-5091, knots-7dda, knots-ee7d); this step confirms acceptance and tags
the branch as the review artifact.

## Acceptance coverage
1. ScopeData (src/domain/scope.rs:49-71) exposes all 10 acceptance fields
   as Option<...> with skip_serializing_if = Option::is_none:
   - volume, scale, volume_score_confidence, volume_stddev, volume_result_id
   - reliability, reliability_score_confidence, reliability_stddev,
     reliability_band, reliability_result_id
   Existing test scope_patch_detects_and_applies_every_field
   (src/domain/scope.rs:158) asserts every field is settable and readable.
2. Legacy knots without scope still load — covered by
   src/sync/apply_tests_legacy_defaults.rs and the default-empty path
   (ScopeData::default().is_empty() -> omitted from view; see
   src/app/types.rs:183).
3. Scope volume is its own field on ScopeData; no pre-existing
   estimate/minutes field on knots is repurposed. Repo scan confirms
   minutes exists only in src/lease_expiry.rs (lease TTL) and
   estimate only as CLI help text in src/cli_scope.rs:10.

## Verification commands run
- cargo test from this branch — 1164 unit tests + integration suites
  pass; full sanity (fmt, lint, tests, coverage) passes at 95.06% with the
  managed pre-push hook.
- kno -C ~/knots fsck — 415 scanned files, 0 issues.

## Notes for the reviewer
- The commit is intentionally empty for source changes — this knot is a
  parent-level acceptance check on work already merged via child knots.
  The commit message contains the verification evidence; the branch
  satisfies the remote_main artifact contract.
- Naming: acceptance text uses the scope_ prefix (e.g. scope_volume);
  domain fields elide that prefix since they live under ScopeData. These
  are semantically equivalent.
